legalFormLocal: Company limited by shares (private) / par value company
documentLocal: JFSC registry search extract / entity search report
summary: The JFSC keeps the public Register of Companies. A registry search returns core company data (name, registered number, status, legal form, date of registration) plus the list of documents filed. This is the register-extract equivalent and is the base document held/issued by the registry.

evidence.quote: every company on the Register, which is not in any winding up process, must submit an annual confirmation statement every year
evidence.note: Confirms the JFSC maintains the public Register of Companies against which searches/extracts are produced.

evidence.basis: Topograph — Business Registers in Jersey (JFSC portal description)
evidence.quote: core company information — name, registration number, status, legal form, and date of registration — is freely searchable

caveats: The free search returns core data; the fuller extract/entity report (with document list) may be a purchasable report rather than free.

legalFormLocal: Company limited by shares (private) / par value company
documentLocal: annual return / annual confirmation statement (Art. 71); register of members (Art. 41/45)
summary: Unusually for a secrecy jurisdiction, LEGAL shareholders ARE evidenced by a registry document in Jersey. The register of members is kept by the company (Art. 41) but the company files an annual return (Art. 71) with the registrar that lists members and their shareholdings; this is on the public record and is routinely sold by data providers as a shareholder document (name, address, number/class of shares). The register of members is also open to inspection by any person on payment (Art. 45). So a current-shareholder document IS obtainable — but only for holders of 1% or more, and it shows REGISTERED (often nominee) holders, not beneficial owners.

evidence.basis: Companies (Jersey) Law 1991, Art. 45(1) (register of members inspection)
evidence.quote: The register of members shall during business hours be open to the inspection of a member of the company without charge, and of any other person on payment

evidence.basis: Companies (Jersey) Law 1991, Art. 71 (annual return) — content summarised from statute
evidence.note: Art. 71 annual return need not identify persons holding less than one per cent of the shares; if full details are not given it must state how many members hold less than one per cent. Paraphrase from statute search — treat as INFERENCE on exact wording.

evidence.basis: SystemDay — Jersey company documents (data provider description of the public search report)
evidence.quote: the name, address, number of shares held by shareholders at date of last return, class of share and any addition remarks
evidence.note: Shareholder detail is derived from the filed annual return and is treated as public.

evidence.quote: Annual Returns: This provides details on shareholders, with their addresses, names, the number of shares, share class, and the secretary

caveats: THRESHOLD: members holding <1% of the share capital need not be individually identified on the annual return — a below-threshold shareholder can be non-public.

NOMINEE/BENEFICIAL: the document shows the REGISTERED member (frequently a nominee), NOT the beneficial owner. Jersey beneficial ownership is held PRIVATELY by the JFSC (restricted OEBO register); no public UBO database.

CONFLICT: since 2021 the 'annual return' was replaced by the 'annual confirmation statement'. Sources disagree on whether the post-2021 statement's member list remains fully public — one guide says private companies are not required to disclose shareholders publicly, while KYC data providers still supply the members list. Verify current retrievability before relying on it.
The register of members itself is company-held, not a registry-issued document; the registry document is the annual return/confirmation statement.

legalFormLocal: Company limited by shares (private) / par value company
summary: The memorandum and articles of association are filed with the registrar at incorporation (uploaded as a single PDF). They constitute the company's constitution (Art. 10) and are obtainable from the JFSC as a certified/purchasable copy, so the constitution is provable from the registry.

evidence.quote: upload the memorandum and articles of association and their subscribers pages as a single PDF document as part of the submission

evidence.basis: Companies (Jersey) Law 1991, Art. 10(1) (effect of memorandum and articles)
evidence.quote: the memorandum and articles, when registered, bind the company and its members to the same extent as if they respectively had been signed
certified online copies costing £90 and physical copies £140
evidence.note: Memorandum & articles listed under purchasable 'Other Company Filings'.

caveats: CONFLICT: one guide states articles are filed at incorporation but not offered as a separate retrievable document through the JFSC portal — conflicting with sources selling certified M&A copies. Retrievability appears real but may require an order/certified-copy request rather than a free download.

documentLocal: JFSC registry search extract / entity search report
summary: Same public Register of Companies applies to public companies. A registry search returns core company data (name, registered number, status, legal form, date of registration) and the filed-document list — the register-extract equivalent, held/issued by the JFSC.

evidence.quote: every company on the Register, which is not in any winding up process, must submit an annual confirmation statement every year

evidence.basis: Companies (Jersey) Law 1991, Art. 4(1)(b) (memorandum states company type)
evidence.quote: whether it is a public company or a private company
evidence.note: Legal-form distinction is recorded in the memorandum and reflected on the register.

caveats: Free search returns core data; fuller extract with document list may be a purchasable report.

documentLocal: annual return / annual confirmation statement (Art. 71); register of members (Art. 41/45)
summary: As for private companies, legal shareholders are evidenced by the annual return filed with the registrar, which lists members and their holdings and is on the public record. The register of members is additionally open to inspection by any person on payment (Art. 45). A current registered-shareholder document is therefore obtainable — subject to the 1% threshold and nominee/beneficial-owner limits.

evidence.basis: Companies (Jersey) Law 1991, Art. 45(1) (register of members inspection)
evidence.quote: The register of members shall during business hours be open to the inspection of a member of the company without charge, and of any other person on payment

evidence.basis: Topograph — Business Registers in Jersey (annual return content)
evidence.quote: Annual Returns: This provides details on shareholders, with their addresses, names, the number of shares, share class, and the secretary
the name, address, number of shares held by shareholders at date of last return, class of share and any addition remarks

caveats: THRESHOLD: members holding <1% need not be individually identified on the annual return — significant for public companies with dispersed holdings, where most holders will be non-public.
NOMINEE/BENEFICIAL: shows REGISTERED members, not beneficial owners; beneficial ownership is held PRIVATELY by the JFSC (restricted OEBO register).
CONFLICT: post-2021 'annual confirmation statement' replaced the annual return; sources disagree on the exact current public shareholder content. Verify live retrievability.

summary: The memorandum and articles are filed with the registrar at incorporation and bind the company and members (Art. 10). They are obtainable from the JFSC as a certified/purchasable copy, so the constitution is provable from the registry for public companies as well.

evidence.quote: upload the memorandum and articles of association and their subscribers pages as a single PDF document as part of the submission
the memorandum and articles, when registered, bind the company and its members to the same extent as if they respectively had been signed
certified online copies costing £90 and physical copies £140

caveats: CONFLICT: one guide states articles are not offered as a separate retrievable document through the JFSC portal, conflicting with sources selling certified copies. Retrievable via certified-copy order rather than necessarily a free download.
